purpose: Promote six WO-015/WO-016 governed-runtime targets into the permanent make ci gate after cold confirmation.
files_modified: Makefile (ci target now includes work-order-parser-check, workflow-grammar-check, execution-plan-check, audit-memory-check, governed-run-check, governed-run-pipeline-check between `test` and `conformance`; summary message updated)
files_NOT_changed_by_design: tests/** (no new tests; no test weakened)

ci_stages_after_promotion.result: PASS — 33 vectors via Python independent re-derivation
PASS — 29 attempts (25 hostile refused, 4 controls allowed)

governed_run_check_result.result: PASS — in-memory governance-flow self-check (valid + refused WO; AUDIT_CHAIN_VALID)

governed_run_pipeline_check_result.result: PASS — 40 pipeline integration tests + 14 CLI tests; 10 of the 40 are macOS-only

platform_behavior.linux_wet_run_status: skipped with explicit reason ("sandbox-exec is macOS only"); not claimed supported

known_limitations: Wet-run is macOS-only because tools/os_isolation_runtime uses sandbox-exec. On non-macOS hosts, the 10 macOS-only tests inside test_governed_run_pipeline.py are skipped with an explicit pytest reason; the other 30 platform-agnostic tests still run. No Linux equivalent is implemented.

Governed-run wet-run dispatch calls tools/os_isolation_runtime.execute_command_isolated directly rather than routing through tools/pipeline_runtime.run() — see reports/runtime_core/governed_run_pipeline_v0.1.md §9 Decision 1 for the integration-decision rationale.

Workflow grammar parser still uses a keyword heuristic. WOs whose prose contains stage synonyms (e.g. "verify" / "execute" outside the workflow list) can be misclassified. Mitigation: test fixtures use prose deliberately free of stage synonyms; real WOs that violate this surface as a no-verify or no-report plan refusal.

CI exercises macOS-only wet-run on this host. A future Linux-hosted CI run will skip the 10 sandbox-exec tests; CI will still PASS on that host because the skipped subset is treated as passing-by-skip per pytest convention. This is documented and intentional, not a silent gap.
remaining_risks: Adding the wet-run pipeline check to ci means every `make ci` run on macOS now spawns real subprocesses under sandbox-exec. Mitigation: three independent gates (WO-015 plan validator → os_isolation_runtime classifier deny-first → macOS sandbox-exec kernel profile), output cap, network deny, fork deny, per-command timeout.

Per-run RefusalRecord files accumulate under <cwd>/intellagent_refusals/ during the wet-run tests. The test harness uses tmp_path so the test-run artifacts are isolated; /intellagent_refusals/ at the repo root is .gitignored.

Sandbox profile files generated during CI (reports/os_isolation_runtime/profiles/*.sb) are tracked in the repo. They accumulate as new commands produce new profiles. This is consistent with the canonical-vs-archived policy in reports/canonical/README.md; periodic archiving via WO-011 patterns remains an option.
next_build_task: Prepare and publish the external verifier recruitment packet (docs/release/THIRD_PARTY_VERIFIER_BRIEF_v0.1.md, docs/release/EXTERNAL_REVIEW_PACKET_v0.1.md, docs/release/REVIEW_QUICKSTART.md) to a non-first-party audience. The in-repo CI gate is now 17 stages strong across Python ref + Python minimal + Rust + Go verifier tracks AND the full governed-runtime kernel; the remaining gap is ownership, not coverage.
